summary: Classified #90980 as the surviving canonical issue for the non-security Docker exec hang/startup-blocking bug. Current main at 6b3e23aba73607c810f119cc0d183e8411ee188b has AbortSignal plumbing in execDockerRaw but no default deadline for sandbox Docker init/start/setup calls, so a wedged Docker CLI can still leave the promise pending. No close or merge action is planned; the safe path is a narrow new fix artifact because #91015 is excluded overlap context and is not a hydrated actionable PR in this job.

actions.reason: #90980 is the only hydrated open actionable ref left after excluded overlap refs, and current main still lacks a bounded Docker exec deadline for sandbox init/start calls.
A narrow non-security fix is still needed on current main, but it must be produced as a cluster fix artifact rather than closing or merging anything in this pass.
Build an executable narrow fix plan so the deterministic fix executor can open a guarded PR after editing, validation, and Codex /review.
